title: SACRAMENTO – Senator Ben Allen (D-Pacific Palisades) celebrated the passage of SB 749 out of the Senate Housing Committee this afternoon to extend affordable housing protections fo
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Mobile homes are the largest source of unsubsidized affordable housing in the nation.
Mobile homes provide important homeownership opportunities for many Californians.
The state's affordable housing funding is oversubscribed.
The existing housing stock is under increasing threat from climate disaster.
SB 749 will expand and clarify existing affordable housing law to protect against the conversion of land from affordable mobile home park units to market-rate units after a disaster.
SB 749 requires park owners seeking to close or change the use of land to provide notice of opportunities to purchase at fair market rates to resident organizations and qualified entities.
Qualified entities that provide notice must have a mission of preserving affordable housing units across California.
